name: Sulista/Gaúcho Portuguese
notes: Sulista / Gaúcho (Rio Grande do Sul, Porto Alegre). Conservative non-palatalising default: /t,d/ stay dental [t,d] before /i/ (the encoded default; a generational shift toward palatalisation is in progress and noted). Distinctive alveolar trill/tap onset /r/ [r~ɾ] — the most European-sounding Brazilian rhotic, NOT glottal; coda /R/ is a tap, not dropped. Reduced final-vowel raising: word-final /e/ often stays [e] in conservative speech. Coda /S/ alveolar. Heterogeneous Italian/German settler substrata. Nascentes (1953:25, O linguajar carioca) places this area in the falares do Sul side of his North/South macro-division (pretonic-vowel openness criterion); Bisol's diphthongization work on /S/-coda (Bisol 1994, 2012), cited in Silva (2014:6-7, ALFAL XVII #4817-4818), draws its impressionistic base specifically from Porto Alegre speech, consistent with the conservative Gaúcho phonology modelled here, though it targets diphthong structure rather than the /r/ and /t,d/ features asserted by this spec.

sources.author: Callou, D. & Leite, Y.
sources.title: Iniciação à fonética e à fonologia (8th ed.)

sources.author: Cardoso, S.A.M. et al.
sources.title: Atlas Linguístico do Brasil (ALiB), vols. 1-2
sources.notes: Geolinguistic atlas; pretonic-vowel and palatalization isoglosses.

sources.author: Noll, V.
sources.title: O português brasileiro: formação e contrastes

sources.author: Silva, A. R.
sources.title: Variação fonética em capitais brasileiras: a ditongação diante de /S/ e as realizações fonéticas do /S/ em coda
sources.publisher: XVII Congreso Internacional ALFAL, João Pessoa (#4813-4818)
sources.notes: Downloaded and read directly (printed conference page headers). Secondary citation of Bisol (1994, 2012) on /S/-coda diphthongization, whose impressionistic data base is Porto Alegre speech (Bisol 1994:125: 'As impressões sobre ditongação em sílabas fechadas por /S/ são baseadas das impressões da autora acerca da fala de Porto Alegre.').

sources.author: Nascentes, Antenor
sources.title: O linguajar carioca
sources.publisher: Organização Simões, Rio de Janeiro (2nd ed.)
sources.notes: Downloaded and read directly. Grounds the North/South macro-division of Brazilian Portuguese dialects on pretonic-vowel openness (p.25); places the Sulista area in the falares do Sul.

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
